title: Message Envelopes, Binary Bodies, Schemas, and Evolution · Compiler Case: Compatible Schema Evolution
summary: Consumers parse Buffer content directly into trusted domain types, overload routing keys with identity data, lack stable event IDs and versions, and break when producers add or remove fields. Planned artifact: Add and verify the compatible schema evolution increment in versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test producer and consumer compatibility for additive, optional, renamed, split, semantic, and removal changes across an overlap window.
Validate unknown envelopes and payloads with strict runtime schemas, bounded collections, semantic refinements, and explicit rejection evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Compatible Schema Evolution · Theory Compatible Schema Evolution
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6497 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Compatible Schema Evolution contract for the supplied data.
